findings.title: /sources/{id}/files endpoint has no API-level test
findings.suggested_fix: Add a test that: (1) uploads a ZIP, refreshes, then GETs /sources/{id}/files; (2) asserts 200, checks returned items have 'file', 'edge_count', and 'confidence' keys; (3) asserts confidence is one of 'high'/'medium'/'low'. Also add a 404 test for a nonexistent source_id.

findings.title: Per-file confidence tier logic (high/medium/low) is never asserted end-to-end
findings.suggested_fix: Test each branch: (a) a clean SQL file with no warnings/approximate edges must return confidence='high'; (b) a file with at least one approximate edge (struct field access) must return confidence='medium'; (c) a file that raises a severity='error' warning must return confidence='low'. Branch (b) is especially important because the logic uses approximate_count > 0 OR warning_count > 0 — a typo could collapse medium into high.

findings.title: Per-statement fault isolation: bad statement drops warning but isolation itself is only tested at parse_sql level, not at engine/graph level
findings.suggested_fix: test_sql_parser.py already tests isolation at parser level (T-564). Add an engine-level test using build_graph_with_warnings with a multi-statement SQL file where one statement is malformed. Assert: (a) the graph contains nodes from the good statements, (b) a ParseWarning is emitted referencing the bad statement. This catches regressions where engine.py might swallow per-statement errors.

findings.title: LATERAL VIEW EXPLODE test does not verify qualified=True and confidence='certain' on emitted edges
findings.suggested_fix: In test_lateral_view_explode_links_output_to_source_array, after asserting 'orders.items' is in item_edge sources, also assert item_edges[0].qualified is True and item_edges[0].confidence == 'certain'. The code emits qualified=True and confidence='certain' for lateral expand edges (sql.py line 501-502) — a regression there would be silent without this check.

findings.title: PIVOT test does not verify that NO edges are emitted for the non-existent pivot columns (no false-positive edge check)
findings.suggested_fix: Add a negative assertion: assert no edge has target_col starting with 'result.__filter__' or 'result.__joinkey__' (verifying pivot does not accidentally emit predicate edges). Also assert that exactly two data edges are emitted for cat_a and cat_b, not more — a bug that emits additional spurious sources would pass the current len-based check.

findings.title: Filter edge test does not verify the filter edge expression field contains the WHERE clause text
findings.suggested_fix: In test_where_clause_emits_filter_edges, add: assert all(e.expression for e in filter_edges), 'filter edges must carry the WHERE expression text'. The production code sets expression=filter_expr_str (sql.py line 405); a regression setting it to None would be undetected.

findings.title: Join key edge test does not verify expression field is populated
findings.suggested_fix: In test_join_on_emits_joinkey_edges, add: assert all(e.expression for e in jk_edges), 'join_key edges must carry the ON clause text'. The code sets expression=on_expr_str (sql.py line 370); a None regression would be invisible.

findings.title: qualified=False flag propagation through _normalize_edges and build_graph is not tested
findings.suggested_fix: Add an engine test: build a graph from a multi-JOIN SQL, then retrieve edges from the graph and assert at least one has qualified=False. Currently test_lineage_edges_expose_qualified_field in test_routes.py only checks that the key exists and is a bool — it does not verify that an ambiguous JOIN actually produces qualified=False in the stored graph edges. A normalization bug that resets qualified=True would pass all current tests.

findings.title: MERGE INTO: neither test verifies transform_type on emitted edges
findings.suggested_fix: In both test_merge_into_matched_update_emits_edges and test_merge_into_not_matched_insert_emits_edges, add assertions on the transform_type of at least one edge. The MERGE parser calls _classify_transform on the RHS expression; a regression returning 'filter' or 'join_key' would be undetected.

findings.title: _split_top_level_statements is not directly unit-tested for semicolons inside string literals
findings.suggested_fix: Add a test for _split_top_level_statements (importable from parsers.sql): pass SQL containing a semicolon inside a string literal, e.g. "SELECT 'a;b' AS x FROM t", and assert it is NOT split into two parts. This is the core correctness claim of the function and currently has zero direct test coverage — only the integration path (parse_sql) exercises it, but with no assertion that semicolons inside strings are correctly skipped.

findings.title: test_one_bad_statement_does_not_drop_good_statements does not assert warning content references the bad SQL
findings.suggested_fix: After asserting warnings_list is non-empty, also assert that the warning string contains something recognisable from the bad statement, e.g. 'NOT VALID' or the file name. The current assertion 'assert warnings_list' only checks that at least one warning was emitted — the warning could contain an unrelated message and the test would still pass.

findings.title: No test for MERGE INTO with USING a subquery (rather than a named table)
findings.suggested_fix: Add: MERGE INTO target USING (SELECT id, val FROM source_table) s ON target.id = s.id WHEN MATCHED THEN UPDATE SET target.val = s.val. The production _parse_merge code checks `isinstance(using_node, exp.Table)` and skips the USING branch otherwise (sql.py line 547). A subquery USING clause silently produces no source attribution — this realistic pattern is completely uncovered.

findings.title: No test for filter edges in a multi-source JOIN (qualified=False / approximate confidence path)
findings.suggested_fix: Add a test: INSERT INTO result SELECT t.val FROM t JOIN s ON t.id = s.id WHERE status = 'active'. Assert a filter edge for 'status' is emitted. Assert confidence='approximate' and qualified=False (because status is unqualified in a multi-source query). Currently the filter edge test uses a single-source query, so the multi_source=True path in the filter block (sql.py line 392) is untested.

findings.title: test_lineage_edges_expose_qualified_field only checks key presence and bool type, not the actual False value for ambiguous JOIN
findings.suggested_fix: Extend the test: after asserting 'qualified' is bool, add an assertion that at least one upstream edge has qualified=False. The SQL used is 'INSERT INTO result SELECT id FROM t JOIN s ON t.id = s.id' — the result.id edge should be qualified=False. Currently a bug that always serialises qualified=True would pass.

residual_risks: MERGE INTO with USING subquery silently emits no source edges (production code explicitly skips non-Table USING nodes); this is a functional gap not just a test gap.

_resolve_temp_views has a subtle bug where src_tbl is compared to temp_views (not temp_views_lower) in the _lookup function (sql.py line 739): 'if src_tbl not in temp_views' should be 'if src_tbl not in temp_views_lower'. This could cause case-sensitive misses when temp view names are uppercase. This may be the cause of any residual phantom-source bugs in case-mixed notebooks.

The confidence tier logic in routes.py refresh_source counts warning_count per file from ParseWarnings, but warning_count is initialised to 0 and incremented per warning (line 248). However, per-statement SQL warnings (from _split_top_level_statements + parse loop) are collected at the file level, not per-statement, so a file with 10 bad statements gets 10 warning_count increments but only appears once in file_stats — this is correct but untested.
useSourceFiles frontend hook has no test coverage (acknowledged in review-context; no unit test framework for frontend hooks in this codebase).

The /sources/{id}/files endpoint returns an empty list (not 404) when the source exists but has never been refreshed (_file_stats is absent). Whether this is the intended contract is unclear and untested.
testing_gaps: Zero API-level coverage for GET /sources/{id}/files (new endpoint, P1).
Zero coverage for confidence tier 'medium' and 'low' branches in refresh_source.
No engine-level test for qualified=False propagation through build_graph_with_warnings.
_split_top_level_statements: no direct unit test for semicolon-inside-string correctness.
MERGE INTO with subquery USING clause: silent zero-edge output, no test.
LATERAL VIEW EXPLODE: transform metadata (qualified, confidence) not asserted.
POSEXPLODE: only value slot tested; pos slot traceability not verified.
